skill.name: flow-finishing-branch
skill.description: Guides developers through branch completion choices, including pull requests, fast-forward merges, squash merges, and cleanup.
skill.summary: Choose a safe completion path for a development branch.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Most static external command hits are Markdown code fences or documented Git examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. The path traversal and weak cryptography detections are false positives, but the skill contains destructive Git cleanup commands and an instruction-like protocol note that tells an agent to check another instruction file. Remove or constrain that protocol note before publication.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection-Like Protocol Instruction
high_findings.description: Line 189 contains an instruction-like protocol note telling the agent to check CLAUDE.md. This can redirect model behavior to external instructions not included in the reviewed skill file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is explicitly formatted as a protocol instruction and asks the agent to load another instruction source. It does not directly say to bypass security review, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Git Cleanup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents branch deletion commands, including forced local deletion and remote deletion. These are legitimate workflow commands but can delete work if executed on the wrong branch.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in the skill and directly modify repository state. The risk depends on user confirmation and branch selection, so this is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are mostly Markdown fenced examples. They are not executable code inside the skill file, although several examples contain real Git commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a Markdown fence boundary or documentation section, not runtime code. The surrounding context shows workflow guidance rather than embedded script execution.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Findings Are Markdown Cross-References
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to relative Markdown links for related documentation. No file access code or dynamic path handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is static relative links in Markdown. There is no command or code path that reads arbitrary files from those paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain description text, workflow prose, or table content. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or encryption API appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not include crypto terms or code. The file is a Markdown workflow guide with no implementation that could perform weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository-Modifying Git Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends commands that change branches, push to remotes, merge pull requests, and delete branches. These should require explicit user confirmation before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete and would alter repository state if executed. They are normal Git operations, but the impact is real when used incorrectly.

content.value_statement: Finishing branches can create missed reviews, messy history, or lost work. This skill gives clear merge, PR, squash, and cleanup choices.
actual_capabilities: Compares fast-forward merge, pull request, squash merge, and cleanup options.
Provides criteria for review needs, file count, and commit history quality.
Includes a pre-completion checklist for tests, build, lint, documentation, and clean working state.
Recommends pull requests as the default when the completion path is unclear.
Documents Git and GitHub CLI commands for common branch completion actions.
limitations: It does not inspect repository state automatically.
It does not verify that tests, builds, or lint checks actually passed.
It assumes Git and GitHub CLI workflows.
It includes destructive cleanup commands that require careful confirmation.

use_cases.title: Finish a Solo Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Choose between a fast-forward merge and a pull request after small isolated work is complete.

use_cases.title: Prepare Team Review
use_cases.description: Select a pull request path when review records, CI checks, and discussion history are needed.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Abandoned Work
use_cases.description: Decide when to delete stale local and remote branches after experiments or changed requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Finish Option
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-finishing-branch to help me decide how to finish my current branch. Ask for the file count, review need, test status, and commit history quality.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Merge
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-finishing-branch to walk through the pre-completion checklist for this branch. Identify any missing checks before I merge or open a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Pick Merge Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-finishing-branch to compare fast-forward merge, pull request, and squash merge for this completed feature. Explain the tradeoffs for audit trail and history quality.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Release Branch Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-finishing-branch as part of a release completion plan. Recommend branch handling, cleanup steps, and confirmations before any repository-changing command is run.

output_examples.input: A small feature changed three files, tests pass, and no review is required.
output_examples.output: Recommended path: fast-forward merge. Confirm the working tree is clean, switch to the main branch, merge only if fast-forward is possible, then delete the completed local branch.

output_examples.input: A production change needs review and CI verification before release.
output_examples.output: Recommended path: create a pull request. Push the branch, open the review, wait for CI, and keep the branch until the pull request is merged.

output_examples.input: An experiment was abandoned after requirements changed.
output_examples.output: Recommended path: cleanup only. Confirm no work needs to be preserved, then remove the local branch and delete the remote branch only after explicit approval.

best_practices: Default to a pull request when review, CI, or audit history matters.
Confirm tests, build, lint, documentation, and working tree state before choosing a merge path.
Ask for explicit confirmation before deleting local or remote branches.
anti_patterns: Do not delete branches before confirming that useful work is preserved.
Do not skip review for production changes only to save time.
Do not squash history when detailed commit history is needed for debugging.

faq.question: Does this skill run Git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents command choices and decision criteria. An agent should ask before running repository-changing commands.

faq.question: When should I choose a pull request?
faq.answer: Choose a pull request for team work, production code, required CI checks, or any change that needs review history.

faq.question: When is fast-forward merge appropriate?
faq.answer: Use it for small, single-developer changes with clean history and no review requirement.

faq.question: What is the main risk of cleanup only?
faq.answer: Cleanup can delete useful work. Confirm the branch is abandoned before deleting local or remote branches.

faq.question: Does it support non-Git workflows?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is written for Git branches and GitHub pull request workflows.

faq.question: Why does the audit recommend review before publication?
faq.answer: The skill includes destructive Git examples and an instruction-like protocol note that should be removed or clearly scoped.
